{{Quote|Your ship registry is originally from the Republic world of Transel. Therefore you are a Republic citizen.|Judge Shelkar, to Revan|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
'''Transel''' was an obscure, backwater terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="SotG" /> located in the [[Transel system/Legends|Transel system]], a part of the [[Southern Core]] portion of the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]]. It was situated on the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] known as the [[Shipwrights' Trace/Legends|Shipwrights' Trace]], which connected it to the [[Bassadro system/Legends|Bassadro]], [[Fondor system/Legends|Fondor]], and [[Surric system]]s.<ref name="TEA Transel" />

A small [[Unidentified Core Galaxy Systems subsidiary|starship engineering company]] was based on Transel. At a single [[Factory/Legends|manufacturing plant]], the firm, which at one point was acquired by the larger [[Core Galaxy Systems]] corporation, for many [[Standard year/Legends|years]] produced the [[Dynamic-class freighter/Legends|''Dynamic''-class freighter]],<ref name="SotG" /> which had been designed by [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]].<ref name="CSWE Ebon Hawk">{{CSWECite|I|224|''Ebon Hawk''}}</ref> By [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="3956 BBY">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' to [[3956 BBY]].</ref> Transel was a member of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
The [[Starship/Legends|starship]] registry of the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'', a ''Dynamic''-class [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] owned by the [[Exchange]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Davik Kang]], was originally from Transel. When the ship's next owner, [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], visited [[Ahto City]] on the neutral<ref name="KOTOR" /> [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> planet [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]]<ref name="KOTOR" /> in 3956 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="3956 BBY" /> the [[Ahto City Civil Authority]] [[Judge/Legends|judge]] [[Shelkar]] informed<ref name="KOTOR" /> him<ref name="TEA" /> of that fact, from which the judge further extrapolated that Revan was a Republic citizen.<ref name="KOTOR" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
Transel was introduced in the [[2003]] [[BioWare]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''. In the game, the planet can be mentioned in an optional conversation the player character, Revan, can have with the character Shelkar. This article assumes one hundred percent game completion and that the conversation took place as described.<ref name="KOTOR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Transel [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square L-13.<ref name="TEA Transel" />
